data.narrative:
Transmutex is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Franklin Servan-Schreiber, Federico Carminati, and Jean-Pierre Revol, having raised $23M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, X-Energy, Oklo, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach, using the $23M total raised as a base value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the subsequent stage. This yields an implied valuation of $46M. Peer-median comparison is not viable due to insufficient granular peer density: while four same-sector nuclear peers exist, none satisfy narrow filters for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier, falling below the three-point minimum threshold required for reliable median anchoring.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a median point of $46M. This range reflects the stage-progression result bounded by ±15% sensitivity margins. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of peer multiples in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector with heterogeneous technology platforms, and reliance on a single triangulation method. Further validation would require proprietary term-sheet data or disclosed fundraising activity from adjacent-stage nuclear peers.
